---
title: "ACE Project plotting"
author: "Caleb"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

# Aceing our understanding of ASE

## Background to idea for project



Three key aspects I wanted to explore relating to ASE (ancestral statee estimation).

* how does fossil sampling influence ASE estimates?
* how does this influence change across different rates of discrete trait evolution/models of continuous trait evolution.
* how does the 'threshold' value / 'fuzzy ace' for collapsing discrete states impact accuracy of ASE.


## Fossils

Fossils are thought to increase the accuracy of ASE by breaking long branches in phylogenetic trees (Slater, 2012; Keating, 2024). I'm interested in the interaction between the transition rate/model of trait evolution and fossil sampling level on the accuracy of ASE.

## Threshold

When estimating discrete ancestral states, a 'threshold' value is used to 'collapse' states as either a point estimate or an uncertainty, or sometimes missing. Often, this method will be too permissive in collapsing states (i.e `Claddis` sets its default threshold as 0.01, whereby if a state has a marginal likelihood higher than other states over this threshold it will be selected). Or alternatively, the threshold is too conservative and deeper node states are all recovered as uncertain (i.e 0/1), which I've found can lead to erroneous inferences in downstream analyses.


## Method

I simulated a set of 100 trees with 50 extant tips and speciation at 1 and extinction at 0.7. This leaves trees with 50 - 200 fossil tips.

Using `map.traits`, I can simulate trait data across each tree whereby each node and tip is assigned a state, which is in effect the **true** evolutionary history.

For discrete traits, I simulated trait data across each tree (100 characters, 85% binary, 15% 3-state) using Mk model with 3 different transition rates: fast = 0.9, medium = 0.1, slow = 0.01.
 
For continuous traits, I simulated both BM and OU model trait data (50 characters). 


I then pruned these matrices/trees of all node characters and tip characters at 5 levels, preserving the root so tree height is maintained.
living - only extant tips
fossil low - 5% fossil coverage and all extant tips
fossil mid - 15% fossil coverage and all extant tips
fossil high - 50% fossil coverage and all extant tips 
all - all tips (extant and fossils)

# ASE

I then ran ase on these matrices with three different 'threshold' methods:

* Default: the ancestral state estimated are all the ones that are have a scaled likelihood greater than the maximum observed scaled likelihood minus the inverse number of possible states (i.e. select_state >= (max(likelihood) - 1/n_states)). If more than one state is over this threshold, it is recovered as uncertain (0/1).
* Threshold = 0.5: will select the ancestral states estimate with a scaled likelihood equal or greater than 0.5
* Sample: `multi.ace` has a `sample = x` parameter, whereby you can return x matrices, which are scaled in terms of states recovered by the scaled likelihood of each state appearing at each node. In this case I used `sample = 10` for fast computation.

To allow for comparisons between different fossil coverages, only the nodes estimated in living were used in subsequent analyses.


```{r echo = FALSE}
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
lenient_means <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/lenientmeans.rds")
relative_majority_rule <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointstrictmeans.rds")
no_majority_rule <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/samplestrictmeans.rds")
strict_majority_rule <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/threshstrictmeans.rds")

root_dist_sample <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/rootdistsamp.rds")
root_dist_default <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/rootdistpoint.rds")
root_dist_thresh <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/rootdistthresh.rds")

morpho_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/morphospacediffs.Rds")
discrete_var_default_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointvardiffs.rds")
discrete_var_sample_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/samplevardiffs.rds")
discrete_var_thresh_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/threshvardiffs.rds")


range_default_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointrangediffs.rds")
range_sample_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/samplerangediffs.rds")
# displ_diffs <-  readRDS("../Data/matrices/dispdiffs.rds")


neighbours_default_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointneighboursdiffs.rds")
neighbours_sample_diffs <-  readRDS("../Data/matrices/sampleneighoursdiffs.rds")

cont_default_dists <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/contpointdists.Rds")
point_dists <- lapply(cont_default_dists, lapply, lapply, median)
sample_dists <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/contsampledists.Rds")


default_ord_diff <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointorddiff.Rds")
sample_ord_diff <-  readRDS("../Data/matrices/sampleorddiff.Rds")


var_point_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/continuous/sumvarpoint.Rds")
var_sample_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/continuous/sumvarsample.Rds")

allnode_means_rel <-  readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/rel_raw_dist.Rds")
allnode_means_thresh <-  readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/thresh_raw_dist.Rds")
allnode_means_sample <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/sample_raw_dist.Rds")


```



## Strict distances

To calculate the accuracy of the ase estimates of discrete traits, I measured the distance for each matching node between true vs ase matrices. Essentially hamming distance, but with no leniency for uncertainty (i.e. uncertainty is penalised). I then calculated the mean distance (somewhere between 0-1) for each matrix at each fossil sampling level.


```{R echo = FALSE, message=FALSE}

# List of levels to loop through
levels <- c("fast", "med", "slow")

# Loop through each level and generate a plot
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)


# Combine data for all levels and replicates into a single data frame
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Combine strict_means and strict_means_sample into a single data frame
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("allnode_means_thresh", "allnode_means_rel", "allnode_means_sample"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        threshold_method = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))
combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("allnode_means_thresh", "allnode_means_rel", "allnode_means_sample"),labels = c("Strict majority rule", "Relative majority rule", "No majority rule (sample)"))


png("../Manuscript/figures/strictdistance.png", width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", res = 600)
# Generate the combined plot with faceting by threshold_method
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 2.5  # Much thicker lines
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ threshold_method, ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Mean Distance Score",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Thicker axis labels and ticks
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),      # Larger, bold axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),     # Larger, bold axis titles
    axis.ticks = element_line(size = 1.2),                   # Thicker axis ticks
    # axis.line = element_line(size = 1.2),                    # Thicker axis lines
    
    # Larger legend
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),                   # Larger legend text
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),   # Larger legend title
    legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "cm"),                       # Larger legend keys
    
    # Facet labels
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16.7, face = "bold"),     # Larger facet labels
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
    scale_y_reverse(limits = c(1.0, 0.0), expand = c(0,0))  # Ensure 0 is included
  dev.off()

```



## Root distance

This is the same as strict distance but the only at the root node.

```{R echo = FALSE}
# library(tidyr)
# library(ggplot2)
root_dist_sample <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/rootdistsamp.rds")
root_dist_default <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/rootdistpoint.rds")
root_dist_thresh <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/rootdistthresh.rds")
# Combine strict_means and strict_means_sample into a single data frame
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("root_dist_thresh", "root_dist_default", "root_dist_sample"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        threshold_method = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))
combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("root_dist_thresh", "root_dist_default", "root_dist_sample"), labels = c("Strict majority rule", "Relative majority rule", "No majority rule (sample)"))

pdf("../Manuscript/figures/rootdistance.pdf", width = 8, height = 4)

ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ threshold_method, ncol = 3) +  # Create 3 panels side by side
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Mean Distance Score",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse() # Reverse the y-axis if needed

dev.off()
```



```{R eval = FALSE, echo=FALSE}
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(morpho_diffs), function(level) {
  # Iterate over replicates within each level
  do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(morpho_diffs[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
    # Extract the data for the current replicate
    data <- morpho_diffs[[level]][[replicate_index]]
    
    # Create a data frame
    df <- data.frame(
      replicate = replicate_index,
      all = data$all,
      high = data$fossil_high,
      mid  = data$fossil_med,
      low  = data$fossil_low,
      living = data$living,
      level = level  # Add a column to indicate the level
    )
    
    # Convert to long format
    df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"), 
                            names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
    df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
    
    return(df_long)
  }))
}))

combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("root_dist_thresh", "root_dist_default", "root_dist_sample"), labels = c("Strict majority rule", "Relative majority rule", "No majority rule (sample)"))
# Generate the combined plot
# Generate the combined plot
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,  # Transparency for the shaded area
    color = NA    # No border for the ribbon
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Distance to True Value"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_reverse()   # Match fill colors to line colors



```

## Sum of variances

I then constructed distance matrices and ordinated both the 'true' and 'aced' matrices, then calculated the difference between the sum of variances values between true and aced.

```{R echo = FALSE}

discrete_var_default_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointvardiffs.rds")
discrete_var_sample_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/samplevardiffs.rds")
discrete_var_thresh_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/threshvardiffs.rds")


library(scales)
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("var_strict_diffs", "var_rel_diffs", "var_sample_diffs"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        threshold_method = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))

combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("var_strict_diffs", "var_rel_diffs", "var_sample_diffs"), labels = c("Strict majority rule", "Relative majority rule", "No majority rule (sample)"))

# log10_reverse_trans <- trans_new(
#   name = "log10_reverse",
#   transform = function(x) -log10(x),  # Apply log10 and reverse
#   inverse = function(x) 10^(-x)      # Inverse of the transformation
# )

png("../Manuscript/figures/allnodessumvardistance.png", width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", res = 600)

# Generate the combined plot
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 2.5  # Thicker lines
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ threshold_method, ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Relative Distance to True Disparity Value",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Thicker axis labels and ticks
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(size = 1.2),
    # axis.line = element_line(size = 1.2),
    
    # Larger legend
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "cm"),
    
    # Larger facet labels
    strip.text = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),  # Bigger facet titles
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse()   # Match fill colors to line colors

dev.off()

```



## Sum of ranges

```{R echo = FALSE}
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("range_default_diffs", "range_sample_diffs"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        treatment = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))

# Generate the combined plot
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level, linetype = treatment)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = interaction(level, treatment)),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = interaction(level, treatment)),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Distance to True Value",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate",
    linetype = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_reverse()  # Reverse the y-axis if needed


```

## Average neighbour distance

```{R echo = FALSE}
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("neighbours_default_diffs", "neighbours_sample_diffs"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        treatment = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))

# Generate the combined plot
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level, linetype = treatment)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = interaction(level, treatment)),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = interaction(level, treatment)),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Distance to True Value",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate",
    linetype = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_reverse()  # Reverse the y-axis if needed


```

## Continuous traits (BM vs OU)

Here, i measured the euclidean distance between scaled BM and OU ace estimates and true values. BM is closer to true value than OU because there model misspecification with OU (ace estimates using BM model). 

```{R echo = FALSE}
# Loop through each level and generate a plot
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)


# Combine data for all levels and replicates into a single data frame
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Combine strict_means and strict_means_sample into a single data frame
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("point_dists", "sample_dists"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        threshold_method = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))

combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("point_dists",  "sample_dists"), labels = c("Point", "Sample"))

pdf("../Manuscript/figures/contdist.pdf", width = 8, height = 4)

# Generate the combined plotcolors <- c("BM" = "purple", "OU" = "darkorange")
colors <- c("bm" = "purple", "ou" = "darkorange")

ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ threshold_method, ncol = 3) +  # Create 3 panels side by side
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +  # Apply custom colors to lines
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +   # Apply custom colors to ribbons
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Distance to True Value",
    color = "Model",  # Updated label to reflect BM/OU
    fill = "Model"    # Updated label to reflect BM/OU
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse() # Reverse the y-axis if needed
dev.off()

```

## Ordinated continuous

I calculated the 'true morphospace', then projected the aced values onto this morphospace and calculated the euclidean distance between corresponding nodes.

```{R echo = FALSE}

combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("default_ord_diff", "sample_ord_diff"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        treatment = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))

# Generate the combined plot
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level, linetype = treatment)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = interaction(level, treatment)),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = interaction(level, treatment)),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Distance to True Value",
    color = "Model",
    fill = "Model",
    linetype = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_reverse()  # Reverse the y-axis if needed


```


## Next steps

Looking at incorporating a mass extinction event... how does survivorship bias in a selective extinction affect ase estimates? Does using sample function improve estimates compared to point estimates? 


## Sum of variances continuous

I then constructed distance matrices and ordinated both the 'true' and 'aced' matrices, then calculated the difference between the sum of variances values between true and aced.

```{R echo = FALSE}


library(scales)
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("var_point_diffs", "var_sample_diffs"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        threshold_method = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))


combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("var_point_diffs",  "var_sample_diffs"), labels = c("Point", "Sample"))

# log10_reverse_trans <- trans_new(
#   name = "log10_reverse",
#   transform = function(x) -log10(x),  # Apply log10 and reverse
#   inverse = function(x) 10^(-x)      # Inverse of the transformation
# )

pdf("../Manuscript/figures/sumvarcontinuous.pdf", width = 8, height = 4)
colors <- c("bm" = "purple", "ou" = "darkorange")

# Generate the combined plot
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun.data = function(y) {
      data.frame(
        y = median(y),
        ymin = quantile(y, 0.25),  # 25th percentile
        ymax = quantile(y, 0.75)  # 75th percentile
      )
    },
    geom = "ribbon",
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = level),
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ threshold_method, ncol = 3) +  # Create 3 panels side by side
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +  # Apply custom colors to lines
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +   # Apply custom colors to ribbons
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Relative distance to True Disparity",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse() # Reverse the y-axis if needed

dev.off()

```
